name: DisplayPipeline
summary: The display pipeline calls events during specific phases of drawing During the drawing of a single frame the events are called in the following order. [Begin Drawing of a Frame] CalculateBoundingBoxCalculateClippingPanesSetupFrustumSetupLightingInitializeFrameBufferDrawBackgroundIf this is a layout and detail objects exist the channels are called in the same order for each detail object (drawn as a nested viewport)PreDrawObjectsFor Each Visible Non Highlighted ObjectPostDrawObjects - depth writing/testing onDrawForeGround - depth writing/testing offFor Each Visible Highlighted ObjectPostProcessFrameBuffer (If a delegate exists that requires this)DrawOverlay (if Rhino is in a feedback mode) [End of Drawing of a Frame] NOTE: There may be multiple DrawObject calls for a single object. An example of when this could happen would be with a shaded sphere. The shaded mesh is first drawn and these channels would be processed; then at a later time the isocurves for the sphere would be drawn.

methods.name: AddClippingPlane
methods.signature: public int AddClippingPlane(Point3d point, Vector3d normal)
methods.summary: Add a clipping plane to be used during the drawing of this frame

returns.doc: index for the added clipping plane

methods.name: AvailableOpenGLVersion
methods.signature: public static uint AvailableOpenGLVersion(out bool coreProfile)
methods.summary: If Rhino is using OpenGL for display, this function will return major.minor version of OpenGL available for this instance of Rhino

params.name: coreProfile
params.doc: If true, OpenGL is being used in "core profile" mode

returns.doc: major version * 10 + minor version For example, OpenGL 4.5 returns 45

methods.name: Clone
methods.signature: public DisplayPipeline Clone(RhinoViewport viewport)
methods.summary: Clones the pipeline. Creates an identical copy of "this" pipeline. Copies all conduits from "this" pipeline to the new pipeline.

returns.doc: The newly cloned pipeline if successful, null otherwise. or failed to close.

methods.name: Close
methods.signature: public bool Close()
methods.summary: Closes the pipeline.

returns.doc: True if the pipeline was closed, false if it was already closed or failed to close.

methods.name: CullControlPolygon
methods.signature: public static bool CullControlPolygon()
methods.summary: Returns a value indicating if only points on the side of the surface that face the camera are displayed.

returns.doc: true if back faces of surface and mesh control polygons are culled. This value is determined by the _CullControlPolygon command.

methods.summary: Draws a single arrow object. An arrow consists of a Shaft and an Arrow head at the end of the shaft.

params.name: screenSize
params.doc: If screenSize != 0.0 then the size (in screen pixels) of the arrow head will be equal to screenSize.

params.name: relativeSize
params.doc: If relativeSize != 0.0 and screen size == 0.0 the size of the arrow head will be proportional to the arrow shaft length.

methods.signature: public void DrawArrowHead(Point3d tip, Vector3d direction, Color color, double screenSize, double worldSize)

params.name: direction
params.doc: Direction in which arrow head is pointing.

params.name: screenSize
params.doc: If screenSize != 0.0, then the size (in screen pixels) of the arrow head will be equal to the screenSize.

params.name: worldSize
params.doc: If worldSize != 0.0 and screen size == 0.0 the size of the arrow head will be equal to the number of units in worldSize.

methods.summary: Draw a set of points with a given radius, style and color.

params.name: points
params.doc: Location of points in world coordinates.

params.name: color
params.doc: Color of points. If style is ControlPoint, this will be the border color.

methods.name: DrawPolygon
methods.signature: public void DrawPolygon(IEnumerable<Point3d> points, Color color, bool filled)
methods.summary: Draws a filled, convex polygon from a collection of points.

params.name: points
params.doc: Collection of world coordinate points that are connected by lines to form a closed shape. Collection must contain at least 3 points.

params.name: filled
params.doc: true if the closed area should be filled with color. false if you want to draw just the border of the closed shape.

methods.name: DrawStereoFrameBuffer
methods.signature: public bool DrawStereoFrameBuffer(ViewportInfo viewportLeft, ViewportInfo viewportRight, out uint handleLeft, out uint handleRight)
methods.summary: Draws the viewport as seen from the left and the right eye viewports and returns the result as OpenGL texture handles.

params.name: viewportLeft
params.doc: The viewport representing the left eye location and look direction.

params.name: viewportRight
params.doc: The viewport representing the right eye location and look direction.

params.name: handleLeft
params.doc: Will contain the OpenGL texture handle which references the left output color buffer.

params.name: handleRight
params.doc: Will contain the OpenGL texture handle which references the right output color buffer.

returns.doc: true if drawing succeeded, false otherwise.

methods.name: EnableClippingPlanes
methods.signature: public void EnableClippingPlanes(bool enable)
methods.summary: Enable or disable the Clipping Plane logic of the engine.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true to enable Clipping Planes, false to disable.

methods.name: EnableColorWriting
methods.signature: public void EnableColorWriting(bool enable)
methods.summary: Enable or disable the ColorWriting behavior of the engine.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true to enable ColorWriting, false to disable.

methods.name: EnableDepthTesting
methods.signature: public void EnableDepthTesting(bool enable)
methods.summary: Enable or disable the DepthTesting behavior of the engine. When DepthTesting is disabled, objects in front will no longer occlude objects behind them.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true to enable DepthTesting, false to disable.

methods.name: EnableDepthWriting
methods.signature: public void EnableDepthWriting(bool enable)
methods.summary: Enable or disable the DepthWriting behavior of the engine. When DepthWriting is disabled, drawn geometry does not affect the Z-Buffer.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true to enable DepthWriting, false to disable.

methods.name: EnableLighting
methods.signature: public void EnableLighting(bool enable)
methods.summary: Enable or disable the Lighting logic of the engine.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true to enable Lighting, false to disable.

methods.name: Flush
methods.signature: public void Flush()
methods.summary: Force the pipeline to immediately flush any cached geometry to the display

methods.name: GetDrawListSerialNumbers
methods.signature: public static void GetDrawListSerialNumbers(out uint modelSerialNumber, out uint pageSerialNumber)
methods.summary: Gets the current model and page view draw list serial numbers, which can be used to determine if a model or page view needs to be redrawn.

params.name: modelSerialNumber
params.doc: The current model draw list serial number.

params.name: pageSerialNumber
params.doc: The current page view draw list serial number.

methods.summary: Get lights that this pipeline is current using

methods.name: GetOpenGLCameraToClip
methods.signature: public float[] GetOpenGLCameraToClip()
methods.summary: Get an array of 16 floats that represents the "camera" to "clip" coordinate transformation in OpenGL's right handed coordinate system

methods.name: GetOpenGLWorldToCamera
methods.signature: public float[] GetOpenGLWorldToCamera(bool includeModelTransform)
methods.summary: Get an array of 16 floats that represents the "world" to "camera" coordinate transformation in OpenGL's right handed coordinate system

methods.name: GetOpenGLWorldToClip
methods.signature: public float[] GetOpenGLWorldToClip(bool includeModelTransform)
methods.summary: Get an array of 16 floats that represents the "world" to "clip" coordinate transformation in OpenGL's right handed coordinate system

methods.name: InterruptDrawing
methods.signature: public bool InterruptDrawing()
methods.summary: Tests to see if the pipeline should stop drawing more geometry and just show what it has so far. If a drawing operation is taking a long time, this function will return true and tell Rhino it should just finish up and show the frame buffer. This is used in dynamic drawing operations.

returns.doc: true if the pipeline should stop attempting to draw more geometry and just show the frame buffer.

methods.name: IsActive
methods.signature: public bool IsActive(RhinoObject rhinoObject)
methods.summary: Determines if an object can be visible in this viewport based on it's object type and display attributes. This test does not check for visibility based on location of the object. NOTE: Use CRhinoDisplayPipeline::IsVisible() to perform "visibility" tests based on location (is some part of the object in the view frustum). Use CRhinoDisplayPipeline::IsActive() to perform "visibility" tests based on object type.

returns.doc: true if this object can be drawn in the pipeline's viewport based on it's object type and display attributes.

methods.name: IsVisible(BoundingBox)
methods.signature: public bool IsVisible(BoundingBox bbox)
methods.summary: Test a given box for visibility inside the view frustum under the current viewport and model transformation settings.

returns.doc: true if at least some portion of the box is visible, false if not.

methods.name: IsVisible(Point3d)
methods.signature: public bool IsVisible(Point3d worldCoordinate)
methods.summary: Test a given 3d world coordinate point for visibility inside the view frustum under the current viewport and model transformation settings.

returns.doc: true if the point is visible, false if it is not.

methods.name: IsVisible(RhinoObject)
methods.signature: public bool IsVisible(RhinoObject rhinoObject)
methods.summary: Test a given object for visibility inside the view frustum under the current viewport and model transformation settings. This function calls a virtual IsVisibleFinal function that sub-classed pipelines can add extra tests to. In the base class, this test only tests visibility based on the objects world coordinates location and does not pay attention to the object's attributes. NOTE: Use CRhinoDisplayPipeline::IsVisible() to perform "visibility" tests based on location (is some part of the object in the view frustum). Use CRhinoDisplayPipeline::IsActive() to perform "visibility" tests based on object type.

returns.doc: true if the object is visible, false if not.

methods.name: MakeDefaultOpenGLContextCurrent
methods.signature: public static bool MakeDefaultOpenGLContextCurrent()
methods.summary: Make a "default" OpenGL context current

methods.name: Measure2dText
methods.signature: public Rectangle Measure2dText(string text, Point2d definitionPoint, bool middleJustified, double rotationRadians, int height, string fontFace)
methods.summary: Determines screen rectangle that would be drawn to using the Draw2dText(..) function with the same parameters.

params.name: definitionPoint
params.doc: either lower-left or middle of text.

returns.doc: rectangle in the viewport's screen coordinates on success.

returns.doc: True if the pipeline was opened, false if it was already open or failed to open.

methods.summary: Remove a clipping plane from the pipeline for this frame

properties.name: DefaultCurveThickness
properties.summary: Gets the curve thickness as defined by the current display mode. Note: this only applies to curve objects, Brep and Mesh wires may have different settings.

properties.name: DpiScale
properties.summary: Scale factor used for high resolution displays. When a monitor that this pipeline is drawing to is at a DPI of 96, this value is one. On high DPI monitors, this value will commonly be greater than one.

properties.name: DrawingGrips
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the pipeline is currently in a grip drawing operation.

properties.name: DrawingSurfaces
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the pipeline is currently in a surface drawing operation. Surface drawing means draw the shaded triangles of a mesh representing the surface (mesh, extrusion, or brep). This is useful when inside of a draw event or display conduit to check and see if the geometry is about to be drawn as a shaded set of triangles representing the geometry. See DrawingWires to check and see if the wireframe representation of the geometry is going to be drawn.

properties.name: DrawingWires
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the pipeline is currently in a curve drawing operation. This is useful when inside of a draw event or display conduit to check and see if the geometry is about to be drawn is going to be drawing the wire representation of the geometry (mesh, extrusion, or brep). See DrawingSurfaces to check and see if the shaded mesh representation of the geometry is going to be drawn.

properties.name: FrameBuffer
properties.summary: Gets the contents of the frame buffer that this pipeline is drawing to.

properties.name: FrameSize
properties.summary: Gets the size of the frame buffer that this pipeline is drawing to.

properties.name: IsDynamicDisplay
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the viewport is in Dynamic Display state. Dynamic display is the state a viewport is in when it is rapidly redrawing because of an operation like panning or rotating. The pipeline will drop some level of detail while inside a dynamic display state to keep the frame rate as high as possible.

properties.name: IsInViewCapture
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether this pipeline is currently drawing for ViewCaptureToFile or ViewCaptureToClipboard

properties.name: IsOpen
properties.summary: Is true of the pipeline is open, false otherwise.

properties.name: IsOpenGL
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this pipeline is drawing into an OpenGL context.

properties.name: IsPrinting
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether this pipeline is currently drawing for printing purposes.

properties.name: IsStereoMode
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether this pipeline is currently using an engine that is performing stereo style drawing. Stereo drawing is for providing an "enhanced 3-D" effect through stereo viewing devices.

properties.name: ModelTransform
properties.summary: Gets or sets the current model transformation that is applied to vertices when drawing.

properties.name: ModelTransformIsIdentity
properties.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether the Model Transform is an Identity transformation.

properties.name: NestLevel
properties.summary: Gets the current nested viewport drawing level. This is used to know if you are currently inside the drawing of a nested viewport (detail object in Rhino). Nest level = 0 Drawing is occurring in a standard Rhino viewport or on the page viewport.Nest level = 1 Drawing is occurring inside a detail view object.

properties.name: RenderPass
properties.summary: Gets the current pass that the pipeline is in for drawing a frame. Typically drawing a frame requires a single pass through the DrawFrameBuffer function, but some special display effects can be achieved through drawing with multiple passes.

properties.name: ShadingRequired
properties.summary: Gets or sets the "ShadingRequired" flag. This flag gets set inside the pipeline when a request is made to draw a shaded mesh but the current render engine doesn't support shaded mesh drawing...at this point the redraw mechanism will make sure everything will work the next time around.

properties.name: StereoProjection
properties.summary: Gets the current stereo projection if stereo mode is on. 0 = left1 = right If stereo mode is not enables, this property always returns 0.

properties.name: SupportsShading
properties.summary: Gets whether or not this pipeline supports shaded meshes.

events.name: CalculateBoundingBoxZoomExtents
events.summary: Calculate a bounding to include in the Zoom Extents command.

events.name: DrawForeground
events.summary: Called after all non-highlighted objects have been drawn and PostDrawObjects has been called. Depth writing and testing are turned OFF. If you want to draw with depth writing/testing, see PostDrawObjects.

events.name: DrawOverlay
events.summary: If Rhino is in a feedback mode, the draw overlay call allows for temporary geometry to be drawn on top of everything in the scene. This is similar to the dynamic draw routine that occurs with custom get point.

events.name: PostDrawObject
events.summary: Called right after an individual object has been drawn. NOTE: Do not use this event unless you absolutely need to. It is called for every object in the document and can slow display down if a large number of objects exist in the document

events.name: PostDrawObjects
events.summary: Called after all non-highlighted objects have been drawn. Depth writing and testing are still turned on. If you want to draw without depth writing/testing, see DrawForeground.

events.name: PreDrawObject
events.summary: Called right before an individual object is being drawn. NOTE: Do not use this event unless you absolutely need to. It is called for every object in the document and can slow display down if a large number of objects exist in the document

events.name: PreDrawObjects
events.summary: Called before objects are been drawn. Depth writing and testing are on.

events.name: PreDrawTransparentObjects
events.summary: Called before transparent objects have been drawn. Depth writing and testing are on.

events.name: ViewportProjectionChanged
events.summary: Called when the projection changes for a viewport being drawn.
